front: What is a stationary time series?
back: A time series is stationary if its statistical properties such as mean, variance, and autocorrelation are constant over time. This implies no trend or seasonality.

front: What is the Autoregressive (AR) model and its general notation?
back: An AR model predicts future behavior based on past values. The AR(p) model is denoted as: X_t = c + φ₁X_{t-1} + φ₂X_{t-2} + ... + φ_pX_{t-p} + ε_t, where p is the order, φ are coefficients, c is a constant, and ε_t is white noise.

front: What is the Moving Average (MA) model and its general notation?
back: An MA model predicts future values based on past forecast errors. The MA(q) model is denoted as: X_t = μ + ε_t + θ₁ε_{t-1} + θ₂ε_{t-2} + ... + θ_qε_{t-q}, where q is the order, θ are coefficients, μ is the mean, and ε_t is white noise.

front: What is the Autoregressive Moving Average (ARMA) model and its general notation?
back: An ARMA model combines both AR and MA components. The ARMA(p, q) model is denoted as: X_t = c + ε_t + Σ_{i=1}^p φ_iX_{t-i} + Σ_{j=1}^q θ_jε_{t-j}, where p is the AR order and q is the MA order.

front: What is the purpose of the Autocorrelation Function (ACF) and Partial Autocorrelation Function (PACF) in identifying linear stationary models?
back: The ACF plots correlation between observations at different lags. The PACF plots the correlation between observations at two lags with the linear dependence of intermediate lags removed. They are used to identify the orders p and q for AR and MA models, respectively (e.g., an AR(p) process has a PACF that cuts off after lag p).
